interpretation: Per-frame detection works but leaves visible frame-to-frame geometric noise.
Plain optical flow reduces translation but increases residual rotation relative to reference tracking.
Best main result on the clean static case.
Residual alignment is accepted but does not improve this already stable sample.
Per-frame detection is sensitive to dynamic page content and produces higher rotation.
Plain flow drifts with scrolling content.
Reference gates many updates and falls back to a stable trajectory; translation metric is affected by actual scrolling content.
Residual alignment is mostly disabled by the global accept-ratio gate
Large screen-internal motion makes detection-only output unstable.
Plain flow follows part of the motion but cannot separate screen plane from content motion.
Reference tracking keeps a strong inlier set and lowers residual rotation.
Residual alignment is not beneficial because the internal video motion dominates the residual measurement.
Failure boundary: moire/high-resolution texture leaves too few reliable reference points.
Strong positive 60fps case with stable reference tracking.
Acceptable dynamic hard case; more residual rotation than static samples but tracking remains reliable.
